steps.prompt: How many finite verbs does this sentence have?

hints.one (est): 'Est' appears twice — once in the relative clause and once in the main clause
hints.three: There are exactly two instances of 'est' and no other verbs
hints.none: Both instances of 'est' are finite verbs

hints.to eat: 'To eat' is 'edere' — 'est' here is from 'esse' (to be)

hints.edo, esse, edi, esum: These are the parts of 'edere' (to eat)
hints.sto, stare, steti, statum: These are the parts of 'stare' (to stand)
hints.eo, ire, ii, itum: These are the parts of 'ire' (to go)

steps.reveal: minimo = in the least / a very small thing (abl., after 'in')

hints.dative: 'Minimo' could be dative, but after 'in' it is ablative of sphere/respect

hints.positive degree: Positive of 'small' is 'parvus' — 'minimus' is the superlative
hints.regular form: 'Minimus' is an irregular superlative of 'parvus' — not a regular form

hints.positive degree: Positive would be 'magnus' — 'maior' is the comparative
hints.regular form: 'Maior' is an irregular comparative of 'magnus' — not formed with -ior from the positive stem

hints.accusative: Accusative would be 'fidelem' — 'fidelis' is nominative, predicate with 'est'
hints.genitive: Genitive would also be 'fidelis' but here it is predicate nominative with 'est'

hints.1st/2nd declension adjective: 1st/2nd decl. adjectives end in -us/-a/-um — 'fidelis/-e' is 3rd declension
hints.4th declension: Adjectives do not follow the 4th declension
hints.indeclinable: 'Fidelis' clearly declines: fidelis, fidelem, fideli, etc.

steps.prompt: What is the logical structure of this sentence?
steps.target: a general truth: faithfulness in small matters implies faithfulness in great ones
choices: a general truth: faithfulness in small matters implies faithfulness in great ones

hints.a conditional contrary to fact: Contrary-to-fact conditions use subjunctive — both verbs here are indicative
hints.a temporal sequence: There is no temporal conjunction — the structure is 'who is X, also is Y'
hints.a purpose clause: Purpose clauses use 'ut/ne' + subjunctive — this is a relative clause + main clause

translation: He who is faithful in the least is also faithful in the greater
